title: polypodium cambricum subsp. macaronesicum
intro: Polypodium cambricum subsp. macaronesicum, also known as Macaronesian polypody, is a subspecies of fern native to the Macaronesian Islands in the North Atlantic. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Polypodium cambricum subsp. macaronesicum include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as potentially exerting protective effects against certain diseases. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts have been used to create topical creams and salves for skin and wound care.
